  • Download Rmd

02_diets_from_stomach_contents

Peter Flood

2025-09-05

Install libraries as needed

if (!require("tidyverse")) install.packages("tidyverse")
if (!require("openxlsx")) install.packages("openxlsx")
if (!require("ggrepel")) install.packages("ggrepel")
if (!require("RVAideMemoire")) install.packages("RVAideMemoire")
if (!require("pander")) install.packages("pander")
if (!require("ggpubr")) install.packages("ggpubr")
if (!require("ggbreak")) install.packages("ggbreak")
if (!require("vegan")) install.packages("vegan")

Load libraries

library(openxlsx) #readaing and exporting Excel files
library(ggrepel) #prevent text from overlapping in ggplot
library(RVAideMemoire) #pairwise PERMANOVA
library(pander) #bold values in cells
library(ggpubr) #arrange multiple plots and 95% confidence ellipses
library(ggbreak) #create breaks in axes while plotting
library(vegan) #multivariate analyses
library(tidyverse) #data manipulation

Read in data

#Using merged stomach counts 
#which is output from previous scripts
stomach.counts <- read.csv("Outputs/Stomach Contents/stomachs_count_merge.csv")

Filter down to herps only

herp.stomachs <- stomach.counts %>% 
  filter(Species %in% c(
    "ACRIPT", #Southern Cricket Frog tadpole
    "NERFLO", #Florida Green Watersnake
    "NOTVIR", #Peninsula Newt
    "PSEAXA", #Everglades Dwarf Siren
    "RANATP", #Pig Frog tadpole
    "RANGRY", #Pig Frog adult
    "SIRLAC" #Greater Siren
  ))

Sample size table

sample.sizes <- herp.stomachs %>% 
  group_by(size_class, Habitat, Season) %>% 
  tally() 

write.csv(sample.sizes, "Outputs/Stomach Contents/stomach_content_sample_sizes.csv")

Multivariate Analyses

Format Data

# 1. Filter out empty stomachs and select relevant covariates
#Using the full stomach content matrix for the NMDS to provide additional context to herps
no.empty.stomachs <- stomach.counts %>%
  filter(Empty == 0) %>%
  select(-c(Species, Year, Site, Length, Wet_Weight, Sex, Empty)) %>%
  remove_rownames() %>%
  column_to_rownames(var = "ID")

# 2. Isolate prey columns
prey.com <- no.empty.stomachs %>%
  select(Amphipoda:Vasc.Plant)

# 3. Assess rare prey groups (for your inspection)
sort(apply(prey.com, 2, sum), decreasing = F)
      Argulus   Centrarchid    Vasc.Plant   Oligochaeta Ephemeroptera   Procambarus Cyprinodontid   Hymenoptera   Trichoptera   Belo.Naucor 
            2             2             2             6            10            11            14            41            59            61 
  Green.Algae          Misc    Coleoptera       Araneae  Palaemonetes    Collembola     Misc.Fish     Hemiptera       Odonata    Periphyton 
           64            64            68            76           100           125           146           177           188           233 
     Copepoda       Diptera   Misc.Invert      Detritus  Hydrachnidia     Amphipoda     Ostracoda      Mollusca    Chironomid     Cladocera 
          294           392           462           510           596           630           907           983          1676          2185 
# 4. Remove rare prey groups
prey.no.rare <- prey.com %>%
  select(-c(Argulus, Centrarchid, Vasc.Plant, Oligochaeta)) #removing those with < 10

# 5. Drop zero rows created by removing rare prey items (keep only rows where rowSums > 0)
prey.no.rare.no.zero <- prey.no.rare %>%
  filter(rowSums(.) > 0)

# 6. Get covariate data for kept samples
post.covariates.no.zero <- no.empty.stomachs %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "ID") %>%
  select(ID:Habitat) %>%
  filter(ID %in% rownames(prey.no.rare.no.zero))

NMDS

set.seed(999) #NMDS uses a randomization process, this allows for reproducibility
post.nmds <- metaMDS(prey.no.rare.no.zero, k=2, distance = "horn", 
                     noshare = 0.1, autotransform = F, plot = T)
Using step-across dissimilarities:
Too long or NA distances: 683832 out of 1027461 (66.6%)
Stepping across 1027461 dissimilarities...
Connectivity of distance matrix with threshold dissimilarity 1 
Data are connected
Run 0 stress 0.2274443 
Run 1 stress 0.2355192 
Run 2 stress 0.2304347 
Run 3 stress 0.2405664 
Run 4 stress 0.2527513 
Run 5 stress 0.2325989 
Run 6 stress 0.2345345 
Run 7 stress 0.2289297 
Run 8 stress 0.2785765 
Run 9 stress 0.2423284 
Run 10 stress 0.2408996 
Run 11 stress 0.2311692 
Run 12 stress 0.2284111 
Run 13 stress 0.2415838 
Run 14 stress 0.2388758 
Run 15 stress 0.2340306 
Run 16 stress 0.2326246 
Run 17 stress 0.2358039 
Run 18 stress 0.2436331 
Run 19 stress 0.2302118 
Run 20 stress 0.2443319 
*** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
     8: no. of iterations >= maxit
     2: stress ratio > sratmax
    10: scale factor of the gradient < sfgrmin
post.nmds

Call:
metaMDS(comm = prey.no.rare.no.zero, distance = "horn", k = 2,      autotransform = F, noshare = 0.1, plot = T) 

global Multidimensional Scaling using monoMDS

Data:     prey.no.rare.no.zero 
Distance: horn shortest 

Dimensions: 2 
Stress:     0.2274443 
Stress type 1, weak ties
Best solution was not repeated after 20 tries
The best solution was from try 0 (metric scaling or null solution)
Scaling: centring, PC rotation, halfchange scaling 
Species: expanded scores based on ‘prey.no.rare.no.zero’ 
#quick visulization
ordiplot(post.nmds, type = "text", main = sprintf("k = 2; stress = %.3f", post.nmds$stress))


#calculate stress values by hand
#reconstruct community distance as in the nmds, accounting for zeros
comm.dist <- metaMDSredist(post.nmds, distance = "horn")
Using step-across dissimilarities:
Too long or NA distances: 683832 out of 1027461 (66.6%)
Stepping across 1027461 dissimilarities...
Connectivity of distance matrix with threshold dissimilarity 1 
Data are connected
#calculate ordination distance
ord.dist <- vegdist(post.nmds$points[,1:2], method = "euclidean")
#extract residuals from a monotonic regression
dhat <- isoreg(comm.dist, ord.dist)
#plot monotonic regression
plot(dhat)

#calculate stress w/ residuals of this regression and ordination distances
reg.stress <- sqrt(residuals(dhat)^2/sum(ord.dist^2))
stress.label <- sprintf("Stress = %.2f", reg.stress*100)
stressplot(post.nmds)
text(1.5, 0.5, stress.label)

NMDS Plot

#write this out to Excel for future use in SIBER
write.csv(post.nmds.consumers, 
          "Outputs/Stomach Contents/Diet/post_nmds_consumers.csv",
          row.names = F)
